Name: Hoppip
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It drifts on winds. It is said that when Hoppip gather in fields and mountains, spring is on the way.

Name: Skiploom
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It blooms when the weather warms. It floats in the sky to soak up as much sunlight as possible.

Name: Jumpluff
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may return this Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Blown by the seasonal winds, it circles the globe, scattering cotton spores as it goes.

Name: Yanma
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It can hover in one spot by flapping its wings at high speed. It flits about to guard its territory.

Name: Yanmega
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Its jaw power is incredible. It is adept at biting apart foes while flying by at high speed.

Name: Wurmple
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Often targeted by bird Pokémon, it desperately resists by releasing poison from its tail spikes.

Name: Silcoon
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack’s damage done to this Pokémon if that damage is 60 or less.
Flavor-Text: It wraps silk around the branches of a tree. It drinks rainwater on its silk while awaiting evolution.

Name: Beautifly
Effects: Search your deck for 3 different types of basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Heal 40 damage from each of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Despite its looks, it is aggressive. It jabs with its long, thin mouth if disturbed while collecting pollen.

Name: Cascoon
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with the Defending Pokémon.
Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It never forgets any attack it endured while in the cocoon. After evolution, it seeks payback.

Name: Nincada
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It grows underground, sensing its surroundings using antennae instead of its virtually blind eyes.

Name: Ninjask
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may search your deck for Shedinja and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Because it moves so quickly, it sometimes becomes unseeable. It congregates around tree sap.

Name: Roselia
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Heal 30 damage and remove all Special Conditions from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The more healthy the Roselia, the more pleasant its flowers’ aroma. Its scent deeply relaxes people.

Name: Roselia
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The more healthy the Roselia, the more pleasant its flowers’ aroma. Its scent deeply relaxes people.

Name: Roserade
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Each of its hands contains different toxins, but both hands can jab with near-fatal power.

Name: Roserade
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may search your deck for any card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Each of its hands contains different toxins, but both hands can jab with near-fatal power.

Name: Maractus
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Arid regions are their habitat. They move rhythmically, making a sound similar to maracas.

Name: Foongus
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: For some reason, this Pokémon resembles a Poké Ball. They release poison spores to repel those who try to catch them.

Name: Vulpix
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: It controls balls of fire. As it grows, its six tails split from their tips to make more tails.

Name: Ninetales
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.
Effects: Does 50 more damage for each Special Condition affecting the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its nine tails are said to be imbued with a mystic power. It can live for a thousand years.

Name: Magmar
Flavor-Text: When it breathes deeply, heat waves form around its body, making it hard to see clearly.

Name: Magmortar
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When launching 3,600 degrees F fireballs, its body takes on a whitish hue from the intense heat.

Name: Ho-Oh-EX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is in your discard pile, you may flip a coin. If heads, put this Pokémon onto your Bench and attach 3 different types of basic Energy cards from the discard pile to this Pokémon.
Effects: Does 20 more damage for each different type of basic Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Magikarp
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: A Magikarp living for many years can leap a mountain using Splash. The move remains useless, though.

Name: Gyarados
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Once it begins to rampage, a Gyarados will burn everything down, even in a harsh storm.

Name: Wailmer
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: On sunny days, it lands on beaches to bounce like a ball and play. It spouts water from its nose.

Name: Wailord
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage times the amount of [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
This Pokémon can’t use Giant Wave during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: The biggest of all Pokémon. It can dive to a depth of almost 10,000 feet on only one breath.

Name: Feebas
Effects: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It is a shabby and ugly Pokémon. However, it is very hardy and can survive on little water.

Name: Milotic
Effects: Search your deck for any 3 cards and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Its lovely scales are described as rainbow colored. They change color depending on the viewing angle.

Name: Spheal
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It rolls across ice floes to reach shore because its body is poorly shaped for swimming.

Name: Sealeo
Flavor-Text: It habitually spins things on its nose. By doing so, it learns textures and odors.

Name: Walrein
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. This Pokémon can’t use Ice Entomb during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: It shatters ice with its big tusks. Its thick blubber repels not only the cold, but also enemy attacks.

Name: Buizel
Flavor-Text: It spins its two tails like a screw to propel itself through the water. The tails also slice clinging seaweed.

Name: Floatzel
Flavor-Text: It is a common sight around fishing ports. It is known to rescue people and carry off prey.

Name: Tympole
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: By vibrating its cheeks, it emits sound waves imperceptible to humans. It uses the rhythm of these sounds to talk.

Name: Palpitoad
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: When they vibrate the bumps on their heads, the can make wave in water or earthquake-like vibrations on land.

Name: Seismitoad
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Echoed Voice attack does 50 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They shoot paralyzing liquid from their head bumps. They use vibration to hurt their opponents.

Name: Alomomola
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The special membrane enveloping Alomomola has the ability to heal wounds.

Name: Mareep
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its fluffy coat swells to double when static electricity builds up. Touching it can be shocking.

Name: Flaaffy
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying weakness and resistance).
Flavor-Text: If its coat becomes fully charged with electricity, its tail lights up. It fires hair that zaps on impact.

Name: Ampharos
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, whenever your opponent attaches an Energy from his or her hand to 1 of his or her Pokémon, put 3 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Effects: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: The tip of its tail shines brightly. In the olden days, people sent signals using the tail’s light.

Name: Electrike
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Using electricity stored in its fur, it stimulates its muscles to heighten its reaction speed.

Name: Electrike
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Using electricity stored in its fur, it stimulates its muscles to heighten its reaction speed.

Name: Manectric
Effects: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Does 20 damage to 1 of your Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It discharges electricity from its mane. It creates a thundercloud overhead to drop lightning bolts.

Name: Manectric
Effects: Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It discharges electricity from its mane. It creates a thundercloud overhead to drop lightning bolts.

Name: Emolga
Effects: Search your deck for 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: They live on treetops and glide using the inside of a cape-like membrane while discharging electricity.

Name: Mew-EX
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can use the attacks of any Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s). (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)
Effects: Move as many Energy attached to your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Dustox
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep, Burned, and Poisoned.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Toxic powder is scattered with each flap. At night, it is known to strip leaves off trees lining boulevards.

Name: Shedinja
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out, your opponent can’t take any Prize cards for it.
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: A discarded bug shell that came to life. Peering into the crack on its back is said to steal one’s spirit.

Name: Drifloon
Effects: Put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Because of the way it floats aimlessly, an old folktale calls it a “Signpost for Wandering Spirits.”

Name: Drifloon
Flavor-Text: Because of the way it floats aimlessly, an old folktale calls it a “Signpost for Wandering Spirits.”

Name: Drifblim
Effects: Does 50 damage times the number of Special Energy cards in your opponent’s discard pile.
Put 4 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: At dusk, swarms of them are carried aloft on winds. When noticed, they suddenly vanish.

Name: Sigilyph
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by Pokémon-EX.
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The guardians of an ancient city, they use their psychic power to attack enemies that invade their territory.

Name: Trubbish
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places.

Name: Garbodor
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, each Pokémon in play, in each player’s hand, and in each player’s discard pile has no Abilities (except for Garbotoxin).
Flavor-Text: It clenches opponents with its left arm and finishes them off with foul-smelling poison gas belched from its mouth.

Name: Gothita
Effects: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order.
Flavor-Text: They intently observe both Trainers and Pokémon. Apparently, they are looking at something that only Gothita can see.

Name: Gothorita
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Starlight is the source of their power. At night, they mark star positions by using psychic power to float stones.

Name: Gothitelle
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Does 20 more damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Starry skies thousands of light-years away are visible in the space distorted by their intense psychic power.

Name: Golett
Effects: Heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The energy that burns inside it enables it to move, but no one has yet been able to identify this energy.

Name: Golurk
Effects: Devolve the Defending Pokémon and put the highest Stage evolution card on it into your opponent’s hand.
During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon has no Weakness.
Flavor-Text: It flies around the sky at Mach speeds. Removing the seal on its chest makes its internal energy go out of control.

Name: Cubone
Flavor-Text: When it thinks of its dead mother, it cries. Its crying makes the skull it wears rattle hollowly.

Name: Marowak
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
If the Defending Pokémon has any Resistance, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: From its birth, this savage Pokémon constantly holds bones. It is skilled in using them as weapons.

Name: Nosepass
Flavor-Text: When endangered, it may protect itself by raising its magnetism and drawing iron objects to its body.

Name: Baltoy
Effects: Your opponent shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck and draws 4 cards.
Flavor-Text: It move by spinning on its foot. It is a rare Pokémon that was discovered in ancient ruins.

Name: Claydol
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: An ancient clay figurine that came to life as a Pokémon from exposure to a mysterious ray of light.

Name: Roggenrola
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They were discovered a hundred years ago in an earthquake fissure. Inside each one is an energy core.

Name: Boldore
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Because its energy was too great to be contained, the energy leaked and formed orange crystals.

Name: Gigalith
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on each of your Benched Pokémon.
This Pokémon does 40 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The solar energy absorbed by its body’s orange crystals is magnified internally are fired from its mouth.

Name: Throh
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
You may do 20 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: When they encounter foes bigger than themselves, they try to throw them. They always travel in packs of five.

Name: Sawk
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Does 70 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Tying their belts gets them pumped and makes their punches more destructive. Disturbing their training angers them.

Name: Stunfisk
Effects: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It conceals itself in the mud of the seashore. Then it waits. When prey touch it, it delivers a jolt of electricity.

Name: Terrakion-EX
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your hand to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Murkrow
Flavor-Text: If spotted, it will lure an unwary person into chasing it, then lose the pursuer on mountain trails.

Name: Honchkrow
Effects: You may have your opponent switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: If one utters a deep cry, many Murkrow gather quickly. For this, it is called “Summoner of Night.”

Name: Houndour
Flavor-Text: It is smart enough to hunt in packs. It uses a variety of cries for communicating with others.

Name: Houndoom
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: The flames it breathes when angry contain toxins. If they cause a burn, it will hurt forever.

Name: Stunky
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It sprays a foul fluid from its rear. Its stench spreads over a mile radius, driving Pokémon away.

Name: Skuntank
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It attacks by spraying a horribly smelly fluid from the tip of its tail. Attacks from above confound it.

Name: Aron
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It usually lives deep in mountains. However, hunger may drive it to eat railroad tracks and cars.

Name: Lairon
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: For food, it digs up iron ore. It smashes it steely body against others to fight over territory.

Name: Aggron
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may discard the top 3 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: While seeking iron for food, it digs tunnels by breaking through bedrock with its steel horns.

Name: Registeel-EX
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 3 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Probopass
Effects: You may move an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It freely controls three small units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force.

Name: Durant
Effects: Put a card from your discard pile on top of your deck.
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Durant dig nests in mountains. They build their complicated, interconnected tunnels into mazes.

Name: Altaria
Ability-Effect: Your [N] Pokémon’s attacks do 20 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: If it bonds with a person, it will gently envelop the friend with its soft wings, then hum.

Name: Rayquaza-EX
Effects: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are Energy cards, attach them to this Pokémon.
Discard all basic [R] Energy or all basic [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage times the number of Energy you discarded.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gible
Flavor-Text: It attacks using its huge mouth. While its attacks are powerful, it hurts itself out of clumsiness, too.

Name: Gible
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It attacks using its huge mouth. While its attacks are powerful, it hurts itself out of clumsiness, too.

Name: Gabite
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It loves sparkly things. It seeks treasures in caves and hoards the loot in its nest.

Name: Gabite
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a [N] Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It loves sparkly things. It seeks treasures in caves and hoards the loot in its nest.

Name: Garchomp
Effects: Discard a Special Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.
Flavor-Text: It is said that when one runs at high speed, its wings create blades of wind that can fell nearby trees.

Name: Garchomp
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It is said that when one runs at high speed, its wings create blades of wind that can fell nearby trees.

Name: Giratina-EX
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on the Defending Pokémon.
Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Deino
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It tends to bit everything, and it is not a picky eater. Approaching it carelessly is dangerous.

Name: Deino
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: They cannot see, so they tackle and bite to learn about their surroundings. Their bodies are covered in wounds.

Name: Zweilous
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: After it has eaten up all the food in its territory, it moves to another area. Its two heads do not get along.

Name: Zweilous
Effects: Attach 2 [D] Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Since their two heads do not get along and compete with each other for food, they always eat too much.

Name: Hydreigon
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a [D] Energy attached to 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Effects: Discard 2 [D] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This brutal Pokémon travels the skies on its six wings. Anything that moves seems like a foe to it, triggering its attack.

Name: Hydreigon
Effects: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The heads on their arms do not have brains. They use all three heads to consume and destroy everything.

Name: Aipom
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It lives high among the treetops. It can us its tail as freely and cleverly as its hands.

Name: Ambipom
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Does 10 damage times the number of cards in your hand.
Flavor-Text: Split into two, the tails are so adept at handling and doing things, Ambipom rarely uses its hands.

Name: Slakoth
Effects: This Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It spends nearly all its time in a day sprawled out. Just seeing it makes one drowsy.

Name: Vigoroth
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its heart beats at a tenfold tempo, so it cannot sit still for even a moment.

Name: Slaking
Ability-Effect: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The world’s laziest Pokémon. When it is lounging, it is actually saving energy for striking back.

Name: Swablu
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It can’t relax if it or its surroundings are not clean. It wipes of dirt with its wings.

Name: Swablu
Flavor-Text: It can’t relax if it or its surroundings are not clean. It wipes of dirt with its wings.

Name: Bidoof
Effects: Both this Pokémon and the Defending Pokémon are now Confused.
Flavor-Text: A comparison revealed that Bidoof’s front teeth grow at the same rate as Rattata’s.

Name: Bibarel
Effects: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon’s attacks. That Pokémon can’t use that attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Both this Pokémon and the Defending Pokémon are now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: A river dammed by Bibarel will never overflow its banks, which is appreciated by the people nearby.

Name: Audino
Effects: Remove all Special Conditions from the Defending Pokémon.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its auditory sense is astounding. It has radarlike ability to understand its surroundings through slight sounds.

Name: Minccino
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: They greet one another by rubbing each other with their tails, which are always kept well groomed and clean.

Name: Bouffalant
Ability-Effect: Any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They charge wildly and headbutt everything. Their headbutts have enough destructive force to derail a train.

Name: Rufflet
Effects: Search your deck for 2 Pokémon with [F] Resistance, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They crush berries with their talons. They bravely stand up to any opponent, no matter how strong it is.

Name: Braviary
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: They fight for their friends without any thought about danger to themselves. One can carry a car while flying.

Name: Devolution Spray
Description: Devolve 1 of your evolved Pokémon and put the highest stage Evolution card on it into your hand. (That Pokémon can’t evolve this turn.)
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Giant Cape
Description: The Pokémon this card is attached to gets +20 HP.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Rescue Scarf
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an attack, put that Pokémon into your hand. (Discard all cards attached to that Pokémon.)
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Tool Scrapper
Description: Choose up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards attached to Pokémon in play (yours or your opponent’s) and discard them.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Blend Energy [G][R][P][D]
Description: This card provides [C] Energy. When this card is attached to a Pokémon, this card provides [G], [R], [P], or [D] Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time.

Name: Blend Energy [W][L][F][M]
Description: This card provides [C] Energy. When this card is attached to a Pokémon, this card provides [W], [L], [F], or [M] Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time.

Name: Ho-Oh-EX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is in your discard pile, you may flip a coin. If heads, put this Pokémon onto your Bench and attach 3 different types of basic Energy cards from the discard pile to this Pokémon.
Effects: Does 20 more damage for each different type of basic Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mew-EX
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can use the attacks of any Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s). (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)
Effects: Move as many Energy attached to your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Terrakion-EX
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your hand to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Registeel-EX
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 3 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

Name: Rayquaza-EX
Effects: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are Energy cards, attach them to this Pokémon.
Discard all basic [R] Energy or all basic [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage times the number of Energy you discarded.

Name: Serperior
Ability-Effect: At any times between turns, heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.
